reasoning: băng appears in 'núi băng' (iceberg = mountain + ice), so băng = ice
bó appears in 'bó hoa' (bouquet = cluster + flower), so bó = cluster
bó hoa = cluster + flower = bouquet
cánh hoa = wing + flower = petal (cánh means wing per hint)
dá appears in 'nước đá' (ice = water + rock) and 'dá lửa' (flint = rock + fire), so dá = rock
dá lửa = rock + fire = flint (a stone used to make sparks)
dá phấn = rock + powder = chalk
đường appears in 'đường vòng' (detour = road + circle), so đường = road
đường vòng = road + circle = detour
hoa appears in multiple flower-related compounds (bouquet, petal, pollen, wreath), so hoa = flower
lửa appears in 'núi lửa' (volcano = mountain + fire) and 'dá lửa' (flint = rock + fire), so lửa = fire
mỡ appears in 'mỡ đường' (to pave the way) and 'mỡ mặt' (to make aware), suggesting a verb meaning to open
mỡ đường = to open + road = to pave the way
mỡ mặt = to open + face = to make aware
núi appears in 'núi băng' (iceberg) and 'núi lửa' (volcano = mountain + fire), so núi = mountain
núi băng = mountain + ice = iceberg
núi lửa = mountain + fire = volcano
nước đá = water + rock = ice (nước means water per hint; ice is used twice as noted)
nước mặt = water + face = tear (as in teardrop)
phấn appears in 'dá phấn' (chalk) and 'phấn hoa' (pollen), both powder-like substances, so phấn = powder
phấn hoa = powder + flower = pollen
vòng appears in 'đường vòng' (detour = road + circle) and 'vòng hoa' (wreath = circle + flower), so vòng = circle
vòng hoa = circle + flower = wreath
mặt appears in 'nước mặt' (tear = water of face) and 'mỡ mặt' (to make aware = to open face), indicating mặt = face
